subject: COVID-19: Statement from Troy Downing
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY TROY DOWNING FOR MONTANA
body:
** STATEMENT FROM TROY DOWNING
------------------------------------------------------------

The effect of the COVID-19 Coronavirus will be felt by nearly every American and we are taking steps to make sure that we have ears to the ground and eyes wide open as information flows and recommendations change while we enter uncharted territory.

I have had numerous conversations with health insurance providers, health care providers, direct primary care providers, and physicians including Blue Cross, the Montana Health Co-Op, and others across the state. The message we are getting is that limiting exposure is prudent and social distancing is helpful in minimizing the potential contact and impact of a viral infection.

We understand that the disease can be deadly to the elderly and those with compromised immune systems. Heather and I will keep those affected in our prayers.

The results of businesses, large and small, closing or reducing service will have a negative effect on our economy and we will keep the small business owners, employees, and their families in our thoughts and prayers.

Here’s what we are doing—on the business side, we have closed the tasting room at our distillery and will do everything we can to protect our employees and our community while still keeping the business going. We encourage small businesses in the service industry to offer alternatives such as gift cards and we encourage our neighbors to help support their local businesses by purchasing gift cards and take-away products to help these businesses navigate unexpected closure and decreased numbers of customers.

On the campaign side, we have suspended meet-and-greet events and will not be attending any group activities until we better understand the trajectory of the virus, its risks, and its effects on our country, state, and community.

I have asked the volunteers on our campaign to spend their volunteer hours on community outreach and assistance when able. I encourage them to help with running errands, delivering goods, doing whatever they safely can to help those in need due to the threat of the virus.

For now, I encourage my family, friends, and community to do what they can to keep themselves safe, to help reduce any contact or spread of the virus, and to keep the small businesses in mind who will struggle through the near-term reductions and closings.

Stay safe and God Bless you.

-Troy

name: Jason Leviskas
subject: Jim Oberweis’ Statement on Win in US Congressional District
body:
*Jim Oberweis’ Statement on Win in US Congressional District*

*Mar. 17, 2020*

*For Immediate Release
*

*Contact: Travis Akin*

*618-303-1108 <618-303-1108> *

Sugar Grove, IL – Jim Oberweis, Republican candidate for Congress in the 14
th District, is issuing the following statement on his victory in the
Republican Primary:

This has been a spirited and well fought campaign. I have been disappointed
in the negative tone that the campaign has taken, but all of that is behind
us. Now is the time for us as Republicans to come together and finish the
mission which is to defeat Lauren Underwood in November.

I thank everyone who came out and voted today, both Republicans and
Democrats, as there are many public health concerns weighing heavily on
people’s minds. I would also like to thank all of my volunteers, family and
staff.

Finally, I thank the election judges, the county clerks and their staff who
work hard every year to make our elections successful but particularly the
work they did today under very stressful conditions with all of the
coronavirus concerns. Thanks to all of them for their hard work.

Our country is in tumultuous times, and with Americans facing a great
series of challenges in these coming weeks and months, I intend to make my
case to the voters of the 14th district that I am the candidate with the
business and life experience we need in DC so our country fully recovers
from this national crisis.

To learn more about Jim Oberweis, log onto https://jim2020.com/.

###

name: MJ Hegar
subject: COVID-19: Our mission
disclaimer_text: Paid for by MJ for Texas
body:
I know right now a lot of us are feeling fear and uncertainty about what COVID-19 means for ourselves and our families as we face down the unexpected. As a mother, I'm feeling a lot of that with you.

|
---
[](https://secure.mjfortexas.com/page/m/64890200/7f51f7bc/4c010744/4ef1fd02/3096756348/VEsH/?g=f7VweDpTVzRduSR3BPpGv5A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/logo?refcode=emaillogo&amount=5")

| | Peter,

I know right now a lot of us are feeling fear and uncertainty about what COVID-19 means for ourselves and our families as we face down the unexpected. As a mother, I'm feeling a lot of that with you.

As a combat veteran, I have my eye on our mission: protecting folks from the spread of this disease and the economic fallout. When the unexpected hits, I've always found it critical to stay focused on solutions, so I want to bring you in on what we know, what we're doing and what we need to complete the mission.

**What we know:**
* You can keep yourself and your community safer by washing your hands often with soap and water for at least 20 seconds, staying home as much as you can right now, and contacting your health care provider immediately if you have symptoms like fever, cough, or shortness of breath.
* Grocery stores remain open and stocked, and you can help your neighbors by only buying what you need.
* The House passed a COVID-19 relief bill, the _Families First Coronavirus Response Act_ , which provides free testing, takes steps to enact emergency paid sick leave, and expands food assistance and unemployment insurance. Unfortunately, the Senate took a weekend recess, but now that they are back I am urging our Texas senators to quickly move this legislation.

**What we 're doing as a campaign:**
* To help flatten the curve on the spread of COVID-19, we have suspended all in-person campaigning.
* We're adapting to the new landscape: we'll continue to get our message out to Texans through phone calls, texting, and more. Because now more than ever, we need to keep fighting for a Senate that prioritizes our health care.
* We're rolling out an online Organizing Bootcamp starting this Sunday (**[sign up here](https://secure.mjfortexas.com/page/m/64890200/7f51f7bc/4c010744/4ef1fd01/3096756348/VEsE/?g=f7VweDpTVzRduSR3BPpGv5A "https://mjfortexas.com/bootcamp/")**), and we'll be back in touch soon with more ways we're connecting with Texans.

**What we need:**
* Elected leaders should put public health over politics and work to pass and implement immediate solutions to increase health care capacity to take care of the sick, including setting up mobile hospitals, ramping up production of life-saving ventilators, and ensuring our front-line health care workers have every health and safety protection we can provide -- especially adequate testing.
* We must take urgent steps to reduce the economic fallout and its impact on Texans -- and prioritize making working families and small businesses whole over big corporations' bottom lines.
* Five million Texans are uninsured, which puts them and all Texans at greater risk during this pandemic. We need a public health insurance option to ensure all Americans can receive health care without facing impossible financial choices.

We're all in this together, and if we all do our part, we can get through this mission, slow further spread of COVID-19, and help our communities recover physically and financially from the effects. Above all else, we need to put the health of Americans above political interests.

We know what our mission is, now let's come together and get it done.

\-- MJ
---

|
---

MJ Hegar was a member of the US Air Force and the Air National Guard. Use of her military rank, job, titles, and photographs in uniform does not imply endorsement from the Department of Defense, Air Force, or Air National Guard.

MJ for Texas
P.O. Box 7156
Round Rock, TX 78683

Contributions or gifts to MJ for Texas are not tax-deductible.

| | Paid for by MJ for Texas

body:
No images? Click here https://hfs.cmail19.com/t/t-e-nhijlud-fuutjhhtj-o/

March 17, 2020 Coronavirus In Tennessee

President Donald Trump, Vice President Mike Pence, and Governor Bill Lee are making the right decisions to help prevent the spread of the Wuhan coronavirus in Tennessee. #TeamHagerty applauds the work of Tennessee's first responders and health care workers, who have been working around the clock to keep Tennesseans safe.

We’ve accumulated resources from TN government agencies and the CDC on how to protect yourself and your loved ones from COVID-19: Click HERE [https://teamhagerty.com/coronavirus/]

See more content like this HERE. [https://twitter.com/BillHagertyTN]

Don't Miss Your Chance To Win!

#TeamHagerty is giving away a St. Patrick's Day t-shirt to our strongest supporters.

However you're celebrating St. Patrick's Day in the confines of self-quarantine and social distancing, enter for a chance to own a one of a kind Team Hagerty shirt HERE. [https://secure.winred.com/teamhagerty/st-patricks-t-shirt]

ICYMI: Read Bill's Op-Ed in The Chattanoogan

Last week, Bill released an Op-Ed in the Chattanoogan addressing the serious issue of human trafficking in Tennessee and across the United States. Since 2007, in Tennessee, there have been 823 human trafficking cases reported, with 93 cases reported in 2019 alone. In his piece, Bill calls for a crackdown on websites and social media accounts that promote human trafficking, ensuring local agencies have the resources they need to educate our community on how to spot human trafficking and report it to law enforcement and violators to be prosecuted to the fullest extent of the law -- including the death penalty. Read Bill's full Op-Ed HERE. [https://www.chattanoogan.com/2020/3/9/405422/End-Human-Trafficking-Support-Death.aspx]

#TeamHagerty In The News

Sandy Still: Hagerty will support religious freedom [https://www.t-g.com/story/2669909.html]

As our next Senator, Bill will continue to stand with President Trump against radical liberals who seek to restrict our opportunities to speak about our faith and important values. President Trump and Bill Hagerty will faithfully stand for our Tennessee values and I proudly stand and support them.

Kenneth Eaton: As U.S. Senator, Hagerty would fight socialist agenda [http://www.williamsonherald.com/opinion/letter-to-the-editor-as-us-senator-hagerty-would-fight/article_fe312fd4-3884-11ea-9b19-c37ec864af64.html]

The Democrats running for president would destroy our economy with their socialist takeover of our health care system. Their plan would cost trillions of dollars and take away the insurance you get at your job. That’s why it’s very important that we send a senator to Washington, D.C., who understands how dangerous this is and who will stop the Democrats’ extremely liberal agenda. I believe that representative is Bill Hagerty, a fiscal conservative running for the United States Senate who is endorsed by President Trump.

Mayor Dean Dickey: [https://www.columbiadailyherald.com/opinion/20200208/letter-to-editor-why-former-mayor-dean-dickey-supports-bill-hagerty] [https://www.columbiadailyherald.com/opinion/20200208/letter-to-editor-why-former-mayor-dean-dickey-supports-bill-hagerty] Why former Mayor Dean Dickey supports Bill Hagerty [https://www.columbiadailyherald.com/opinion/20200208/letter-to-editor-why-former-mayor-dean-dickey-supports-bill-hagerty]

In Tennessee, Bill Hagerty is the only U.S. Senate candidate that has been endorsed by President Trump. As President Trump’s former Ambassador to Japan, I know that Bill is ready to hit the ground running on Day 1 to stand with the President to fight back against the Democrats’ radical liberal agenda. He is not afraid to stand up to Nancy Pelosi, “The Squad” and RINOs like Mitt Romney. President Trump backs Bill Hagerty, and so do I.

Be a Part of #TeamHagerty TELL US WHY YOU SUPPORT BILL HAGERTY [https://teamhagerty.ivolunteers.com/Surveys/tell-us-why] DONATE TO THE CAMPAIGN [https://secure.winred.com/teamhagerty/eoq-november?utm_source=housefile&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=EOM%20Nov%202] VOLUNTEER WITH #TEAMHAGERTY [https://teamhagerty.ivolunteers.com/Register/Registration] Press Highlights

Chattanoogan: [https://www.chattanoogan.com/2020/3/9/405422/End-Human-Trafficking-Support-Death.aspx] Hagerty: End Human Trafficking, Support Death Penalty For Traffickers - And Response

TN Star: [https://tennesseestar.com/2020/03/13/bill-hagerty-end-human-trafficking-support-death-penalty-for-traffickers/] Bill Hagerty: End Human Trafficking, Support Death Penalty for Traffickers

TN Star: [https://tennesseestar.com/2020/03/12/tennessee-senate-candidate-bill-hagerty-talks-about-plans-to-protect-our-constitutional-rights-trumps-visit-and-the-death-penalty-for-human-traffickers/] Tennessee Senate Candidate Bill Hagerty Talks About Plans to Protect Our Constitutional Rights, Trump’s Visit, and the Death Penalty for Human Traffickers

Crossville Chronicle: [https://www.crossville-chronicle.com/news/local_news/hagerty-seeks-support-in-u-s-senate-race/article_75fd21aa-62fe-11ea-bccd-8327b27538df.html] Hagerty seeks support in U.S. Senate race

What They're Saying...

"After looking Bill in the eye, I can fully vouch for the fact that he is a pro-life, Second Amendment custodian in full support of our president’s agenda. Without hesitation, I intend to support and vote for him and humbly ask that you do as well."

- [https://www.johnsoncitypress.com/Letter-to-the-Editor/2020/02/14/Letters-It-s-election-season.html] Jeff Broyles [https://www.johnsoncitypress.com/Letter-to-the-Editor/2020/02/14/Letters-It-s-election-season.html]

"I know that Bill Hagerty will work with President Trump, just as he did while Ambassador to Japan, to protect our farmers and make sure that they are treated fairly when it comes to trade. He has my support to be Tennessee’s next Senator."

-Mayor Rick Graham [https://www.williamsonhomepage.com/opinion/letter-to-the-editor-bloomberg-s-comments-on-farmers-insulting/article_80d5c59e-5b3f-11ea-a467-f7cdc0bb2990.html]

"Bill Hagerty is running for the United States Senate to combat the radical socialists and their agenda. I know Bill will stand with President Donald Trump to protect my conservative values and the conservative values of so many Tennesseans across the state."

-Michael Tedder [https://www.roanecounty.com/content/support-hagerty%E2%80%99s-us-senate-bid]

"I know that Bill Hagerty is a good quality Republican. He knows the value of conservative judges and is going to fight to support President Donald Trump. The President and I will continue to come back and support Bill Hagerty."

-Nikki Haley [https://twitter.com/TeamHagerty/status/1229833970914938881]

"That’s why it’s very important that we send a senator to Washington, D.C., who understands how dangerous this is and who will stop the Democrats’ extremely liberal agenda. I believe that representative is Bill Hagerty, a fiscal conservative running for the United States Senate who is endorsed by President Trump."

-Kenneth Eaton [https://teamhagerty.com/letter-to-the-editor-as-us-senator-hagerty-would-fight-socialist-agenda/]

"I am supporting Bill Hagerty for Senate because he will continue fighting for family-owned farms, like mine, to ensure we have the resources necessary to compete on a global scale and win."

-Charlotte Kelley [https://teamhagerty.com/covington-leader-kelley-just-like-president-trump-bill-hagerty-will-deliver-on-his-promises-for-tennesseans/]

"Tennessee loving Bill Hagerty, who was my Tennessee Victory Chair and is now the very outstanding Ambassador to Japan, will be running for the U.S. Senate. He is strong on crime, borders & our 2nd A. Loves our Military & our Vets. Has my Complete & Total Endorsement!"

-President Donald Trump [https://twitter.com/realDonaldTrump/status/1149779387077791749]

Team Hagerty

subject: Time to Flatten the Curve

The curve below shows the number of cases through last night: When we put our collective minds to it, America can do anything. Heck, we put men on the moon with less technology than what I'm typing on right now. [It's time to flatten that curve](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/16223025/172106338/1107445447?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDUvMS83NDEzOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxZmU0OWI0Mi0xOTY5LWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwM2I1ZGQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=xQxgRsMicvvTRAOl-sJiFtjIU9sqbTDSuTHMCsh7cgE=&emci=2d567a1d-0669-ea11-a94c-00155d03b5dd&emdi=1fe49b42-1969-ea11-a94c-00155d03b5dd&ceid=10438639). If you want or need extensive information, [head to the CDC site here](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/16223028/172106341/-1368851855?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDUvMS83NDEzOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxZmU0OWI0Mi0xOTY5LWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwM2I1ZGQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=xQxgRsMicvvTRAOl-sJiFtjIU9sqbTDSuTHMCsh7cgE=&emci=2d567a1d-0669-ea11-a94c-00155d03b5dd&emdi=1fe49b42-1969-ea11-a94c-00155d03b5dd&ceid=10438639). Local, county, state, and the federal officials are all taking steps to encourage, and in some places mandate social distancing and minimize the impacts to our lives and livelihoods. But ultimately, it's up to all of us to collectively make this happen. And let's recognize the heroes of the day. First responders, health care providers, state and local health departments, scientists racing to develop therapeutics and vaccines, anybody working in the food supply chain, and so many more are all putting themselves on the line. name: My Faith Votes
subject: Election Watch: Biden takes the lead, and Coronavirus takes over
body:
| | Keeping Christian voters informed on important election developments!
---
[ ](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=6_nuz-gPbh4AR2HufX66ieBy60TvLGnOIGi3DxU1rjlS2gfivznXOo2VQj2oHvWUTvjSPlN5dHdxSXdSeOAYuafoZVT-9f6513_2RIs0RRc&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e200318com01&n=1)
| Peter, The economic impact of the reactions to the coronavirus will certainly be disruptive and perhaps significant. Sports arenas have shut down. Amusement parks, movie theaters, schools, churches -- all closing. Businesses will be affected and nearly every individual in the country on some level will be impacted. **[While the nation focuses on COVID-19 (the coronavirus) and rightly so, what’s happening with the presidential election campaign](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=6_nuz-gPbh4AR2HufX66ieBy60TvLGnOIGi3DxU1rjlS2gfivznXOo2VQj2oHvWUTvjSPlN5dHdxSXdSeOAYuafoZVT-9f6513_2RIs0RRc&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e200318com01&n=2)?** It was just two weeks ago that Bernie Sanders seemed to be the heavy favorite to win the Democratic nomination for President. Well, not anymore. Things can change quickly in politics... and in life.

South Carolina proved to be the big win Joe Biden needed, and three days later, that momentum pushed him to victories in most of the Super Tuesday states. A week later, he won most of the states again. Looking at the states yet to vote, pundits agree it would take a minor miracle for anyone but Joe Biden to be the eventual nominee.

It seems that Democrats have turned away from Bernie Sanders and the socialist option he was championing. However, a close examination of Joe Biden reveals he has adopted many of the policies of his party’s left-wing. Joe Biden appears to have the nomination well in hand, but the coronavirus has paralyzed the campaigns and the nation. Let’s take a closer look at what’s happening. [](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=6_nuz-gPbh4AR2HufX66ieBy60TvLGnOIGi3DxU1rjlS2gfivznXOo2VQj2oHvWUTvjSPlN5dHdxSXdSeOAYuafoZVT-9f6513_2RIs0RRc&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e200318com01&n=3) **United. We Stand.** [](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=FvZODUdzRCV4_QVEoUI5Y_LYDGbWPUdf00keax2EMv8&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e200318com01&n=4)

subject: Keeping us safe
body:
Keeping us safe | | | [View In Your Browser](https://mailchi.mp/nancymace.org/breaking-a-huge-endorsement-1897549?e=f1831b4ca2) [Forward To A Friend](http://us7.forward-to-friend.com/forward?u=8ef1646525944f29311be74d4&id=3257377179&e=f1831b4ca2)
---
| |
---
| | Dear Friends,

In South Carolina, **14 new cases **were announced yesterday bringing our** total to 47 cases total **throughout 13 counties** **(**3 in Charleston County, 4 in Beaufort County**).** 1 fatality. **This is serious.
**_We're all in this together._**
I encourage each of us to come together to take the necessary precautions to keep our families safe and prevent overloading our health care system to ensure those who need medical attention can get the services they need.

Governor McMaster is putting safeguards in place to keep us safe by limiting the opportunity for community spread:
* Closing all bars and restaurants' dine-in services
* Pushing back state tax deadlines to June
* Banning gatherings of 50+ in public facilities
* Mobilizing the National Guard

As for next steps? Governor McMaster requests the following:
* Halting of elective and non-threatening procedures in surgical centers for 3 days
* Insurance companies cover 100% of visits to the doctor related to coronavirus
* Limit paper product purchases, water, and more by grocery stores
* Allow employees to work from home (where feasible)

If you have a moment, please WATCH and SHARE our PSA below on how to be prudent and safe during COVID-19:
---
| [ ](https://nancymace.us7.list-manage.com/track/click?u=8ef1646525944f29311be74d4&id=f14bd5faca&e=f1831b4ca2)
---
Be informed. Be prudent. #FlattenTheCurve
| I will be in an emergency session in the SC House of Representatives TOMORROW to fund $45 million from the surplus in the state's Contingency Reserve Fund.

History tells us our nation has defeated severe diseases, illnesses and outbreaks. And we will do it again.

Having grown up here, I’ve seen a few natural disasters and devastating events, and South Carolina has always been resilient.

We are strong. And we will get through this together.

So today, I also ask that we each set aside our differences. The time to be political is not now.

Please continue to be informed, be prudent and be safe.

Sincerely,

[](https://nancymace.us7.list-manage.com/track/click?u=8ef1646525944f29311be74d4&id=14b19a4aa3&e=f1831b4ca2)
---
| **RESOURCES:**
* [Centers for Disease Control and Prevention](https://nancymace.us7.list-manage.com/track/click?u=8ef1646525944f29311be74d4&id=1f6466b88a&e=f1831b4ca2)
* [S.C. Department of Health and Environmental Control (DHEC) website](https://nancymace.us7.list-manage.com/track/click?u=8ef1646525944f29311be74d4&id=869eea3855&e=f1831b4ca2)
* DHEC Care Line: [1-855-472-3432](tel:1-855-472-3432) (staff are answering COVID-19 calls 8AM-6PM every day)
* FREE SCREENINGS at [musc.care](https://nancymace.us7.list-manage.com/track/click?u=8ef1646525944f29311be74d4&id=a140039989&e=f1831b4ca2)

---
| **SCDHEC Recommendations**
SCDHEC encourages the public to follow recommendations for social distancing to protect the health and safety of all South Carolinians. This includes:
* Wash your hands frequently (20 seconds)
* Cover your cough
* Stay home when you’re sick
* Avoid public gatherings
* Appropriately dispose tissues and other items that you've sneezed or coughed into
* Practice social distancing

---
| **KNOW THE SYMPTOMS**
* Fever: 88%
* Dry cough: 68%
* Fatigue: 38%
* Coughing up sputum, or thick phlegm, from the lungs: 33%
* Shortness of breath: 19%
* Bone or joint pain: 15%
* Sore throat: 14%
* Headache: 14%
* Chills: 11%

subject: I want to be honest with you
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Booker for Kentucky
body:
It’s been weeks since we learned about the severity of the situation and we’re still waiting for the Senate to pass COVID-19 relief efforts. If this isn’t a sign that we need to change our politics, then I don’t know what is.
#
I know I’m not the only one who’s been feeling upset or affected by this growing crisis — so I want to be honest with you.
These past couple of days have been frustrating.
In times like these, we should be relying on our leaders to guide us and protect our communities. Instead, we’ve been hearing a lot of empty promises and excuses for their lack of action.
**It’s been weeks since we learned about the severity of the situation and we’re still waiting for the Senate to pass COVID-19 relief efforts. If this isn’t a sign that we need to change our politics, then I don’t know what is.**
[**It’s frustrating, but I’m choosing to channel my anger into action. Will you chip in $5 if you agree it’s time to create a new kind of politics that puts people first?**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/16238618/172244151/-561360945?refcode=em20200318&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83ODg4NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwMWJlY2YxMy00YzY5LWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwM2I1ZGQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=-00uL_JxSt77udWAfKMoC9GxVOU__1FE4H3Gji9NeIQ=)

If this isn’t a sign that we need to change our politics, then I don’t know what is. I know I’m not the only one who’s been feeling upset or affected by this growing crisis — so I want to be honest with you. These past couple of days have been frustrating. In times like these, we should be relying on our leaders to guide us and protect our communities. Instead, we’ve been hearing a lot of empty promises and excuses for their lack of action. It’s been weeks since we learned about the severity of the situation and we’re still waiting for the Senate to pass COVID-19 relief efforts. If this isn’t a sign that we need to change our politics, then I don’t know what is. = = History will remember what we did in this moment of crisis. History will remember who fought to make sure every American has access to health care. And history will remember the movements that worked to create change when we most needed it. We need to stick together and focus on the progressive values that brought this campaign together. What we do and how we choose to channel our energy these next couple of weeks will send a huge message about the strength of our people-powered movement and the future of our politics. = Proud to be in this fight with you, Charles = | | ---|---|--- Paid for by Booker for Kentucky

# **Mike Pence: We Need the American People's Help to Fight Coronavirus Now**

“The president and I are deeply proud that the American people have already pulled together in so many ways in response to this virus, just as we’ve always done during other challenging times in our history,” Vice President Mike Pence writes in _USA Today_.

“Now is a critical time. As the president said on Monday, over the next two weeks, there are actions that every American can and should take to help us slow the spread of the coronavirus. If we succeed in limiting the number of new cases, then we can dedicate more resources to the most vulnerable and save lives.”

[Click here to read more.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=8e5beabdf2&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
| “Medicare said Tuesday it will immediately expand coverage for telemedicine nationwide to help seniors with health problems stay home to avoid the coronavirus. The new option will allow millions of older people to take care of ongoing medical problems as well as new concerns, while heeding public health advice to stay home during the outbreak,” Ricardo Alonso-Zaldivar reports for [_The Associated Press_](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=b9cdf017f2&e=3ee4985eea).
---
|
---
| FDA Commissioner Dr. Stephen Hahn warns that while the FDA is working full speed to help diagnose, treat, and prevent the Coronavirus, there are currently no approved treatments on the market. The FDA and its partners will continue warning consumers of fraudulent schemes and “taking action to stop health scams,” he writes in [_USA Today_](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=2ae537baf4&e=3ee4985eea).
---
|
---
| “The Chinese Communist Party’s decision to expel journalists from China and Hong Kong is yet another step toward depriving the Chinese people and the world of access to true information about China”—including about the origin of the Coronavirus, the National Security Council said in a statement yesterday. Read more from Ryan Saavedra in [The Daily Wire](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=18f3d3352e&e=3ee4985eea).

name: Senator Chris Van Hollen
subject: Senate Update: Coronavirus and Telephone Town Hall Tonight
body:
| | | [Click here to open this e-mail in its own browser window](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet//view_newsletter_setup.aspx?link=SenVanHollen%2501143397%2501Senate%2bUpdate_%2bCoronavirus%2bLatest_%2bTown%2bHall%2bTomorrow%250115564.14998763.4466365.7517223%2501dpwillis67%2540gmail.com%2501Friend%2501dpwillis67%2540gmail.com%2501Silver%2bSpring%252c%2bMD%2b20906%2501) | [Click here to open a plain text version of this email](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet//view_newsletter_setup.aspx?plain_text=Y&link=SenVanHollen%2501143397%2501Senate%2bUpdate_%2bCoronavirus%2bLatest_%2bTown%2bHall%2bTomorrow%250115564.14998763.4466365.7517223%2501dpwillis67%2540gmail.com%2501Friend%2501dpwillis67%2540gmail.com%2501Silver%2bSpring%252c%2bMD%2b20906%2501)
---|---
| | | **March 18, 2020 ** |
---|---|---|---
| | | Marylanders and all Americans are concerned about the coronavirus.

I want to let you know what I'm working on in the Senate and across the state to help our communities and protect our residents. And you're invited to join me for a Telephone Town Hall I am hosting with Dr. Leana Wen. We will update you on the fight against the coronavirus, both on the federal and state level. To join us, **dial 855-962-1138 or[join online](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15564.14998763.4466365.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fvideo.teleforumonline.com%2fvideo%2fstreaming.php%3fclient%3d19100&redir_log=250485840253450) at 6:30PM EST tonight (Wednesday).**

There are three main ways we are working to bolster our response to coronavirus: providing the resources our health care system needs to treat the sick and reduce the virus' spread; coordinating across all levels of government to ensure resources and information are getting into the hands of those who need them; and directly helping families that are struggling to buy food and pay bills due to this crisis.

The legislation we passed two weeks ago was a critical "Phase 1" to shore up public health systems, provide funds to states, and enable a strategic government response (more on that bill below).

Now, after days of unnecessary delay, the Senate is finally poised to act on the "Phase 2" legislation passed by the House. This bill is an essential step to ensure free coronavirus testing, provide paid sick leave so that workers can stay home when they are ill, expand food assistance, and support emergency unemployment insurance. It will allow many workers to pay the rent and expenses even if they lose their job - an urgent matter given ongoing layoffs in the hospitality industries.

While the Phase 2 package is not perfect, getting it done will be an important step forward. And we're already at work on a much larger Phase 3 package of additional emergency measures that will get assistance to small businesses, especially in the hospitality industry, and help folks not covered by the previous legislation. This is an urgent matter. We should pass Phase 2 today and work to complete Phase 3 before the end of this week.

As the Senate acts, I'm staying in continuous contact with health care leaders across our state, and federal officials involved in the response, to ensure that we're taking care of the most vulnerable members of our community. You can find more details on all this below and at [my coronavirus website](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15564.14998763.4466365.7517223&report_id=Van+Hollen+coronavirus+website&redirect=http%3a%2f%2fwww.vanhollen.senate.gov%2fcoronavirus&redir_log=992765187082310 "Van Hollen coronavirus website").

For medical information, regularly visit the [CDC coronavirus website](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15564.14998763.4466365.7517223&report_id=CDC+coronavirus+website&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.cdc.gov%2fcoronavirus%2f2019-ncov%2findex.html&redir_log=104035161607358 "CDC coronavirus website").

For information on how the virus is affecting our state, visit the [Maryland DOH coronavirus website](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15564.14998763.4466365.7517223&report_id=Maryland+DOH+coronavirus+website&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fcoronavirus.maryland.gov%2f&redir_log=238126695297234 "Maryland DOH coronavirus website"). And if you feel ill or have individual questions, contact your health care provider.

We've all been frustrated with aspects of the federal response, including the slow ramp up of testing for the virus nationwide. We are working to make tests more widely available and make it easier for people who need testing to get it. But let's be clear, we find ourselves needing to make up for lost time.

Before the crisis, the Trump Administration dismantled the White House global health security and biodefense office specifically established to prepare for and quarterback the response to pandemics. Early in the outbreak, Trump's complacency allowed the virus to get an 8-to-10 week head start. For way too long, the President downplayed the crisis and engaged in dangerous happy talk. He must allow health care officials to lead the response and keep the public informed.

We're headed in the right direction now, with Congress working in a bipartisan way with health care and economic officials at all levels of government to address this crisis. Communities across our country are pulling together to help each other and stay safe. As we each do what we need to do to respond effectively and slow the spread of the virus in our communities, I'll continue to keep you updated.

Chris Van Hollen
### Still here to serve you: A note on how my offices are operating
To follow the guidance of public health officials and ensure the safety of constituents and staff due to COVID-19, the majority of my staff will be teleworking until further notice. As a result, our offices will be closed to the public, but all phone lines will remain fully open and fully staffed. Constituents seeking assistance can reach our constituent services teams at: (301) 545-1500. Those with general questions or concerns about federal policy can reach our Capitol Hill team at: (202) 224-4654. Urgent issues for my office can be emailed to [assistance@vanhollen.senate.gov](mailto:assistance@vanhollen.senate.gov).
### Providing the resources for a strategic response to treat the sick and limit the virus' spread
I fought to pass an $8.3 billion emergency funding package in Congress, which the President signed into law on March 6. That initial [comprehensive package](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15564.14998763.4466365.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.vanhollen.senate.gov%2fnews%2fpress-releases%2fvan-hollen-statement-on-senate-passage-of-coronavirus-supplemental-funding&redir_log=992524885919902) provides our state and local governments with financial support to address this public health emergency. It contains critical resources for the research, development, and approval of a potential vaccine, treatments, and diagnostics - work that is being done at Maryland's NIH and FDA facilities.

Additionally, the legislation provides funding to support preparedness at our hospitals and health centers, to bolster their capacity to handle coronavirus on the frontlines. Lastly, the bill includes critical initial support for small businesses suffering financial losses due to the outbreak - a provision I worked to include after hearing the concerns of Maryland small business owners.

Since passing the "Phase 1" funding package, I've worked to ensure that any [vaccines or treatments developed for COVID-19 are affordable and accessible to all](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15564.14998763.4466365.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.facebook.com%2fwatch%2f%3fv%3d489061058647678&redir_log=76000404128296).
### Ensuring resources and information are getting to those who need them
Maryland announced its first cases of COVID-19 on March 5, 2020. On that day, my Maryland Congressional colleagues and I [announced $500,000 in federal funding to support our state's response](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15564.14998763.4466365.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.vanhollen.senate.gov%2fnews%2fpress-releases%2fmaryland-congressional-delegation-announces-500000-in-federal-funding-to-support-state-level-coronavirus-response&redir_log=615931600071165) \-- and [we just got $10.2 million more](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15564.14998763.4466365.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.vanhollen.senate.gov%2fnews%2fpress-releases%2fmaryland-congressional-delegation-announces-102-million-in-federal-funding-for-maryland-coronavirus-response&redir_log=899423364541135).

The next day, March 6, I visited the National Institutes of Health to hear directly from the scientists working to develop a potential COVID-19 vaccine, and [spoke to the public to share what I learned](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15564.14998763.4466365.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2ftwitter.com%2fChrisVanHollen%2fstatus%2f1236022663149162501&redir_log=498993286021372).

Since then, I have been in contact with Governor Hogan, the Maryland Department of Health, state health care leaders and county and local officials to ensure they're getting the support they need.

Multiple federal agencies have a role in combating the virus. I've worked with my colleagues on actions such as [asking the Department of Health and Human Services to keep the public informed on its response](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15564.14998763.4466365.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.vanhollen.senate.gov%2fnews%2fpress-releases%2fvan-hollen-murray-democrats-press-health-department-for-updates-on-novel-coronavirus-call-for-continuing-robust-science-driven-response&redir_log=582642840561332); pressing [the Food and Drug Administration (FDA) to protect our food and medicine, and its employees](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15564.14998763.4466365.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.vanhollen.senate.gov%2fnews%2fpress-releases%2fvan-hollen-colleagues-urge-fda-to-provide-answers-on-coronavirus-impact-on-inspections-employees&redir_log=082541715591057); and [asking the FDA about how it's working to prevent potential drug shortages related to coronavirus](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15564.14998763.4466365.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.vanhollen.senate.gov%2fnews%2fpress-releases%2fvan-hollen-colleagues-ask-fda-about-potential-drug-shortages-related-to-coronavirus&redir_log=787359700918962).

Many Marylanders work for the federal government and I've acted to protect them by [urging the President to immediately maximize telework for federal employees](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15564.14998763.4466365.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.vanhollen.senate.gov%2fnews%2fpress-releases%2fvan-hollen-democratic-senators-urge-trump-to-immediately-maximize-telework-for-federal-employees&redir_log=179029303881057), building on my earlier [request to the Social Security Administration, a major employer in Maryland, to maximize telework](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15564.14998763.4466365.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.vanhollen.senate.gov%2fnews%2fpress-releases%2fvan-hollen-cardin-brown-press-social-security-administration-on-telework-policies&redir_log=12879507434953). I've also [called on OPM to guarantee protections for federal workers and contractors complying with coronavirus response](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15564.14998763.4466365.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.vanhollen.senate.gov%2fnews%2fpress-releases%2fvan-hollen-senators-call-on-opm-to-guarantee-protections-for-federal-workers-and-contractors-complying-with-coronavirus-response&redir_log=402617676351287), and [pushed Metro to prioritize employee and rider safety during this crisis](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15564.14998763.4466365.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.vanhollen.senate.gov%2fnews%2fpress-releases%2fmaryland-and-virginia-senators-urge-wmata-to-prioritize-employee-and-rider-safety-amid-coronavirus-threat&redir_log=190726128551118).
### Directly helping families that are struggling due to this crisis
Workers should be able to stay home if they're sick, need to be with a child home from school, or need to care for a sick family member -- and they should be able to do so without fearing they'll lose their job or paycheck. Individuals laid off due to the coronavirus need to be able to quickly access unemployment insurance payments. These are among the measures I have been pushing for and I was pleased they were included in the _Families First Coronavirus Response Act_ , which is now poised to pass the Senate. Still, we must dramatically expand on both the paid leave provisions and the unemployment provisions in that bill. For example, workers in the gig economy are not adequately protected by the unemployment compensation systems, and unemployment benefits must last at least as long as the coronavirus persists. And we must help small businesses who have lost customers because of the coronavirus and can't pay their workers or cover expenses. Moreover, we must implement a host of other measures, including my request that financial regulators [help mitigate the economic impact of the coronavirus on workers in the hospitality and tourism industry](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15564.14998763.4466365.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.vanhollen.senate.gov%2fnews%2fpress-releases%2fvan-hollen-cardin-warner-kaine-urge-financial-regulators-to-take-measures-to-protect-hospitality-and-tourism-industry-workers-during-coronavirus-outbreak&redir_log=547476217511466). I have also [called on other employers to offer flexibility due to the coronavirus](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15564.14998763.4466365.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.vanhollen.senate.gov%2fnews%2fpress-releases%2fvan-hollen-senators-call-on-employers-to-offer-flexibility-in-the-event-of-coronavirus-outbreak&redir_log=868777619781045). To help Marylanders struggling to pay the bills, I [pushed to get financial institutions to waive fees and provide extra time to pay loans for consumers who may be suffering financially due to the virus](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15564.14998763.4466365.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.vanhollen.senate.gov%2fnews%2fpress-releases%2fvan-hollen-warner-banking-democrats-urge-regulators-to-provide-guidance-to-financial-institutions-about-forbearance-due-to-coronavirus-impacts&redir_log=563787886731381). To help our state's small businesses, I [encouraged Governor Hogan to make use of federal resources to help restaurants, small manufacturers, and other firms with coronavirus preparedness & response](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15564.14998763.4466365.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.vanhollen.senate.gov%2fnews%2fpress-releases%2fvan-hollen-cardin-urge-hogan-to-enlist-federal-resources-to-help-maryland-small-businesses-with-coronavirus-preparedness-and-response&redir_log=307437588031318). These are among the many additional measures we must take in the coming days. For a full list of my actions to address the coronavirus, visit [vanhollen.senate.gov/coronavirus](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15564.14998763.4466365.7517223&report_id=&redirect=http%3a%2f%2fvanhollen.senate.gov%2fcoronavirus&redir_log=562382490017282).

clean_body: | | | | ---|--- | | | March 18, 2020 | ---|---|---|--- | | | Marylanders and all Americans are concerned about the coronavirus. I want to let you know what I'm working on in the Senate and across the state to help our communities and protect our residents. And you're invited to join me for a Telephone Town Hall I am hosting with Dr. Leana Wen. We will update you on the fight against the coronavirus, both on the federal and state level. To join us, dial 855-962-1138 or at 6:30PM EST tonight (Wednesday). There are three main ways we are working to bolster our response to coronavirus: providing the resources our health care system needs to treat the sick and reduce the virus' spread; coordinating across all levels of government to ensure resources and information are getting into the hands of those who need them; and directly helping families that are struggling to buy food and pay bills due to this crisis. The legislation we passed two weeks ago was a critical "Phase 1" to shore up public health systems, provide funds to states, and enable a strategic government response (more on that bill below). Now, after days of unnecessary delay, the Senate is finally poised to act on the "Phase 2" legislation passed by the House. This bill is an essential step to ensure free coronavirus testing, provide paid sick leave so that workers can stay home when they are ill, expand food assistance, and support emergency unemployment insurance. It will allow many workers to pay the rent and expenses even if they lose their job - an urgent matter given ongoing layoffs in the hospitality industries. While the Phase 2 package is not perfect, getting it done will be an important step forward. And we're already at work on a much larger Phase 3 package of additional emergency measures that will get assistance to small businesses, especially in the hospitality industry, and help folks not covered by the previous legislation. This is an urgent matter. We should pass Phase 2 today and work to complete Phase 3 before the end of this week. As the Senate acts, I'm staying in continuous contact with health care leaders across our state, and federal officials involved in the response, to ensure that we're taking care of the most vulnerable members of our community. You can find more details on all this below and at "Van Hollen coronavirus website". For medical information, regularly visit the "CDC coronavirus website". For information on how the virus is affecting our state, visit the "Maryland DOH coronavirus website". And if you feel ill or have individual questions, contact your health care provider. We've all been frustrated with aspects of the federal response, including the slow ramp up of testing for the virus nationwide. We are working to make tests more widely available and make it easier for people who need testing to get it. But let's be clear, we find ourselves needing to make up for lost time. Before the crisis, the Trump Administration dismantled the White House global health security and biodefense office specifically established to prepare for and quarterback the response to pandemics. Early in the outbreak, Trump's complacency allowed the virus to get an 8-to-10 week head start. For way too long, the President downplayed the crisis and engaged in dangerous happy talk. He must allow health care officials to lead the response and keep the public informed. We're headed in the right direction now, with Congress working in a bipartisan way with health care and economic officials at all levels of government to address this crisis. Communities across our country are pulling together to help each other and stay safe. As we each do what we need to do to respond effectively and slow the spread of the virus in our communities, I'll continue to keep you updated. That initial provides our state and local governments with financial support to address this public health emergency. | | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/16241802/172275548/664205141?refcode=em031820-header&refcode2=9b2cb94e-5769-ea11-a94c-00155d03b5dd&noskip=true&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDcvMS83NDI2NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5YjJjYjk0ZS01NzY5LWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwM2I1ZGQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Bt9RzcBOQcRBlnP4Aqw6rTRk-r6tumtJ-JRa1Cyj0HY=) **** | | Friend –

We have a crisis of leadership in the federal government that is being exposed by the coronavirus global pandemic. We need steady, experienced leaders that will use every tool at the government’s disposal to meet this crisis on the frontlines.

**[I’ll say more in just a moment, but right now our campaign is up against a MAJOR fundraising deadline. We’re in danger of falling short if online donations slow down. body:
---
We’ll keep this brief, Peter.

While folks are worried about keeping themselves and their loved ones healthy and safe, worried about how they’re going to keep a roof over their heads after their jobs are cut, and worried about taking care of their children while schools are closed, **Steve King is posting memes on Facebook about the coronavirus pandemic.**
| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/16244070/172300500/473856631?refcode=em20_200318_stevekingrr&noskip=true&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDEvMS82MTU4MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2MjdjODNjMy02MTY5LWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwM2I1ZGQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=86JRs0GpECEyZvOM_7u-hOl5imLVhtH2oiFoHDi1McA=)
---

In times of crisis, you see people’s true colors. Some folks rise to the occasion and lead. And then there’s Steve King... who stokes fear, makes light of the situation, and pushes conspiracy theories.

**[Peter, are you ready for new leadership for Iowa’s 4th District?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/16244071/172300501/473856631?refcode=em20_200318_stevekingrr&noskip=true&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDEvMS82MTU4MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2MjdjODNjMy02MTY5LWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwM2I1ZGQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=86JRs0GpECEyZvOM_7u-hOl5imLVhtH2oiFoHDi1McA=)**

[I'M READY](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/16244072/172300502/473856631?refcode=em20_200318_stevekingrr&noskip=true&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDEvMS82MTU4MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2MjdjODNjMy02MTY5LWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwM2I1ZGQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=86JRs0GpECEyZvOM_7u-hOl5imLVhtH2oiFoHDi1McA=)

Stay safe, friends. And Steve King -- stay off Facebook.

name: Mike Bloomberg
subject: Millions of lives
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Mike Bloomberg 2020
body:
Responding to the coronavirus the right way.

| Friend, The coronavirus pandemic has shown us just how connected we are, from our neighborhoods and cities to our global community. We are in this together -- and our response has to match that reality. That's why yesterday I shared two important efforts to accelerate the fight against the coronavirus, at the local and global levels: **The launch of the Coronavirus Global Response Initiative**
* This $40 million initiative will support immediate action and prevent or slow the spread of the coronavirus in vulnerable low- and middle-income countries.
* Bloomberg Philanthropies will partner with global health organizations to support lower-income countries and cities as they respond to the coronavirus pandemic.

**The first virtual convening of the Coronavirus Local Response Initiative**
* Building on an announcement from last week, more than 180 city leaders will join public health experts tomorrow to receive the most up-to-date information on the virus, and crisis coaching to manage the public health pandemic.
* The virtual convening will be the first in an ongoing series to provide city leaders with technical assistance and coaching, and the chance to share emerging practices with other mayors on the front lines of the public health crisis.

**Millions of lives depend on getting our coronavirus response right -- and so does the economic and social health of communities around the world. Can you help us share the message that we're in this together?** | [SHARE ON FACEBOOK »](http://i.mikebloomberg.com/u/click?_t=8613ca2a3316453b8f979e9fa5797d01&_m=9ecf87b13bc24b21b27980791285218c&_e=Qh3RqqtQP6KM7jtXfeG-0iPK8k78oO3ggY7KdFmarXgRJImQTMT8ID2Ixo9xFrH2Axp9Uh-tXgNliDb1LhZ449Hhg3ZCMi_3xUv4vygRxiuwhC-VBX4hBBtVMbDkZG-mkSwWei8Id4pnmoI_inBi_9fxe5CoEDq_k83rcjgimSyi4UFTirIAzR8MqpzdtoX3XbeIe_rwkdBUx5mjIIa16tbDrCZ9LGSDmYygPwbMCKWt4kbJoYcFKucx6lShnsk1K3P83oi2_L48J1zdqZ46lhBWuihqX4EuUN0XmvNpBrV7dqO-ug4DrKkPbyLge2B7-RQWu1bOu3HwGBCiZ7nyKJm5nWdk6-LyKVC0fj9dUJmJQd4GB6kIMrIrUvovqLZSBbZosX9mK3l6yAofxG2eG6TpTYqw2hQOpwvTJyCWS-aPek9hoVavi65LByTdCWTs)

name: Tim Kaine
subject: Details on staying safe
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kaine for Virginia
body:
| | _If you have reason to believe you have been exposed to the coronavirus and you have a fever and symptoms of a respiratory illness, you should call your health care provider immediately. _
* * *
Derek: The outbreak of the novel coronavirus that we are facing can create uncertain and stressful times. I wanted to take a moment today to email you about what I'm doing in the Senate to address this pandemic, what you can do yourself, and what we can all do together as a community. First, as the government works to confront the thread of COVID-19, I will not stop pushing for maximum transparency and close collaboration with health experts and scientists. We must ensure that those who need to be tested and treated are able to be, and that the public is given accurate information. I'm also pushing to make sure the Senate immediately passes the bipartisan legislative package that passed the House this weekend. This legislation works to support American workers and families throughout this crisis -- it provides paid sick leave to workers, guarantees free coronavirus testing, increases federal funds to hospitals, and expands unemployment insurance. It doesn't address everything, but it's an important step, and I am doing everything I can in Washington, D.C. to help get it through. I also want to make sure you have information about what you should be doing at home to stay safe and help reduce the spread of the coronavirus. I urge every American to follow health safety guidelines, no matter what. **This is the latest guidance from the White House and the CDC for everyone, even the young and otherwise healthy, effective immediately through the end of March: **
* Work (and school) from home whenever possible.
* Avoid social gatherings of more than 10 people.
* Avoid eating and drinking at restaurants or bars, and instead opt for delivery or takeout if you're not able to cook at home.
* Avoid unnecessary travel, shopping, and social visits.
* Older people and those with underlying conditions should stay home and away from others as much as possible.
* Stay home if you are feeling sick, and keep your kids home from school if they're feeling sick.
* And always practice good hygiene, washing your hands frequenty, with soap and water, for a full 20 seconds, and avoiding touching your face.

**Public health experts have made this very clear: We must all take this pandemic seriously and do our part to protect ourselves and those around us. ** Information from the CDC is evolving rapidly as the situation changes. [**Coronavirus.gov**](https://secure.timkaine.com/page/m/330f1705/3e0906/78dc121f/3e30c65c/2810541171/VEsH/?g=_j7eKVH8x8VayGMy1IzDoYA "http://coronavirus.gov?utm_medium=email&utm_source=kaine&utm_content=1+-+Coronavirusgov&utm_campaign=20200318_TK_COVID_19&source=20200318_TK_COVID_19") is a valuable resource that is updated regularly. It's also important to check the news and follow guidance from your state and local elected officials. Finally, I know that many of these social distancing and protective measures that we are rightly taking can feel isolating. Some people aren't able to work remotely -- including, of course, our medical professionals and many of those serving the community in grocery stores, pharmacies, and other essential businesses. But if you are at home and feeling lonely, uncertain, or anxious, I encourage you to take some measures to protect your mental health. This can include walks in the outdoors at a safe distance from others (six feet or more), checking in on loved ones by phone or video call, and taking time to read a good book and escape the news, however briefly. Thank you for reading, and please stay safe. -Tim

---

name: Bill Weld
subject: the cause endures
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Weld for President 2020 Presidential Campaign Committee · United States
body:
|
---
Friend — I wanted to tell you first: I have decided to suspend my candidacy for President of the United States, effective immediately. I am intensely grateful to all the patriotic women and men who have stood with me and supported me during the past eleven months in our effort to bring better government to Washington, D.C.**** Since announcing my candidacy less than a year ago, tens of thousands of supporters and donors like you all across the nation joined the cause, working day and night to promote strong, experienced, decent leadership for the United States. You are determined, creative, and indefatigable in your efforts to give Americans a better choice in the 2020 Presidential election. ******Leading this movement is one of the greatest honors of my life** , and I will always be indebted to all who have played a part. But while I am suspending my candidacy, **I want to be clear that I am not suspending my commitment to the nation and to the democratic institutions that set us apart.** America is truly the greatest country on earth. It’s up to each of us to ensure that it remains so. **** Forever grateful, Bill

name: =?utf-8?Q?Dani=20Brzozowski?=
subject: We did it!
body:
For the first time ever, more Democrats voted in the primary than Republicans in IL 16 |
---
| |
---
| |
#
Last night we made history

**For the first time** since Adam Kinzinger was first elected, **Democratic primary voters in IL-16 outnumbered Republicans.** And not by a little. By a lot. Voters showed up in droves to make sure the message is loud and clear: Kinzinger is out.

We have a long way to go until November, and under the rapidly-evolving circumstances of campaigning amidst the COVID-19 pandemic, we will have a lot to learn. But**I couldn’t be prouder or more humbled by the work you’ve all put into this campaign** so far and by the support it’s so obvious this movement has.

We are fighting for **you**. For Medicare for All. For education. We’re fighting to expand social security and sweeping action to address climate change. We’re fighting so that, when faced with a crisis, this district, and the rest of the country, has the kind of leadership we deserve. _The kind of leadership that feels competent and compassionate_. The kind of leadership that works, and works for you.

Help us get there. The message is strong, the voters are making themselves heard, but we know it’s going to take consistent drum-beating from now until November. [Make a $15, $50, or $100 contribution today to help us amplify our message and keep this momentum going.](https://daniforillinois.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=16d8d19eb3a417b5b09061c0d&id=da9c9a4e8f&e=140a9b81e7)

Dani Brzozowski

#
_We know this is a difficult time, so please take care of yourself and your loved ones and only pitch in if you can afford to do so. To pause our emails until this public health emergency has passed,[fill out this form](https://secure.hickenlooper.com/page/m/6377131b/691c9bd/68afb402/11a7636a/408949634/VEsH/?g=Wvb-QcQot1BcsCb456pUfvA "https://jhfp.cp.bsd.net/page/signup/covid-19?source=em200318")._ Folks -- I want to be honest with you for a moment, if you can spare a few minutes to let me explain something. Here it goes: These are not normal times. It would be wrong to pretend they are. Our nation is facing an unprecedented public health emergency that is impacting virtually every facet of American society right now. Our campaign has canceled all in-person events, including our fundraisers, and it means we're engaging in a different kind of campaign. But although the tactics are different, our campaign's message is not: **This crisis is a reminder that we need to come together more than ever to get things done. That 's how we improved our state's economy here in Colorado coming out of the Great Recession, and it's how our country will survive the COVID-19 outbreak, too.** I am writing to you today to ask you to contribute to our campaign if you believe our country is ready to put aside our partisan blinders and get to work on the critical issues that matter, from addressing this pandemic to climate change and building an economy that works for everyone. I am asking you to chip in today, but I only want you to do it if you are financially able to. I really mean that, folks. name: Team Warner
subject: update on coronavirus
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Mark Warner
body:
This afternoon, Mark voted to pass an emergency bipartisan coronavirus relief bill that guarantees free coronavirus testing, expands unemployment benefits, preserves school breakfast and lunch program
|
---
| | Derek --

As the coronavirus (COVID-19) continues to spread in Virginia, we want to update you on the work Mark Warner is doing in the Senate to deal with the coronavirus outbreak and share some health precautions we all should take to protect our communities.

There are now 67 confirmed cases of COVID-19 in the Commonwealth, and Mark is working hard in the Senate to make sure that Virginians and folks across the country are protected. **[He's been outspoken on the fact that this is not the time for the Senate to take a break or delay legislation](https://act.markwarnerva.com/page/m/7e85d566/53d9897e/532d0dd1/1c8997c8/3413669835/VEsH/?g=fQCtPq_HJ6FdDGwS5GeylnA "https://www.msnbc.com/andrea-mitchell-reports/watch/sen-warner-i-don-t-think-we-should-have-gone-out-of-session-on-friday-80714821918")** -- they have a responsibility to keep working hard for the American people.

Here is what Mark Warner is working on in the Senate:
* This afternoon, Mark voted to pass an emergency bipartisan coronavirus relief bill that guarantees free coronavirus testing, expands unemployment benefits, preserves school breakfast and lunch programs, increases Medicaid funding, and provides other targeted relief for Americans impacted by the coronavirus.
* Earlier this month, Mark helped pass an $8.3 billion emergency coronavirus response bill that directed millions in needed resources for Virginia's coronavirus response. That package includes provisions the senator personally wrote to increase access to critically important tele-health technology.
* Going forward, Mark is focused on passing additional targeted economic relief for American workers, because he believes no one should miss a paycheck or lose their job because of the coronavirus outbreak.

As he works to pass laws to protect us in the Senate, we've got to do our part to protect our own communities. That means following the **[Center for Disease Control's Coronavirus guidelines](https://act.markwarnerva.com/page/m/7e85d566/53d9897e/532d0dd1/1c899737/3413669835/VEsE/?g=fQCtPq_HJ6FdDGwS5GeylnA "https://www.cdc.gov/coronavirus/2019-nCoV/index.html"),** including washing your hands often and avoiding sharing personal items and beverages. It's hard to be apart from those we love, but the steps that we take to practice social distancing now could be critical in slowing and ending the spread of COVID-19.

Help us spread the word -- not the virus. Here are two actions you can take right now to make sure that your friends and family are well-informed and that you're up to date on the latest coronavirus news:
* **Follow sources of reliable information.** We recommend following the **[CDC](https://act.markwarnerva.com/page/m/7e85d566/53d9897e/532d0dd1/1c899736/3413669835/VEsF/?g=fQCtPq_HJ6FdDGwS5GeylnA "https://twitter.com/CDCgov")** and **[Virginia Department of Health](https://act.markwarnerva.com/page/m/7e85d566/53d9897e/532d0dd1/1c899735/3413669835/VEsC/?g=fQCtPq_HJ6FdDGwS5GeylnA "https://twitter.com/VDHgov")** for up-to-date health information. We will also be posting the latest news about cases in Virginia and resources for Virginians through the Team Warner **[Twitter](https://act.markwarnerva.com/page/m/7e85d566/53d9897e/532d0dd1/1c899734/3413669835/VEsD/?g=fQCtPq_HJ6FdDGwS5GeylnA "https://twitter.com/MarkWarnerVA")** and **[Facebook](https://act.markwarnerva.com/page/m/7e85d566/53d9897e/532d0dd1/1c899733/3413669835/VEsA/?g=fQCtPq_HJ6FdDGwS5GeylnA "https://www.facebook.com/MarkWarnerVa")** accounts.
* **Forward this email to 3 people.** We're committed to keeping this list informed about the latest coronavirus news in the Commonwealth and will be sending updates at least once a week.

We will get through this together if everyone does their part to slow and stop the spread of the virus. We will continue to provide regular updates to this list.

Be well,

-- Team Warner
---
|
---

subject: Together, America will prevail
body:
“We are at war with an invisible enemy, but that enemy is no match for the spirit and resolve of the American people.” | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=847d9ef833&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| The White House • March 18, 2020
---
| | |
# **Together, America will prevail**

**“I want all Americans** to understand,” President Trump wrote on Twitter this afternoon. “We are at war with an invisible enemy, but that enemy is no match for the spirit and resolve of the American people.”

**He’s right**. Thanks to the dedication of our doctors, nurses, and scientists—as well as the love, patriotism, and determination of our citizens—no country is better prepared to meet this challenge than America. No nation on Earth will emerge stronger and more united than ours.

**The Federal Government** is leaving no stone unturned in its unprecedented response. Today, the President announced more groundbreaking steps to slow the spread of the virus and help every American community brave the storm:
* The Defense Department will **massively expand medical resources** , making 5 million respirator masks and 2,000 ventilators available for use

* FEMA is now **activated in every region** of the country and at the highest level

* The Department of Housing & Urban Development will be **suspending foreclosures and evictions** until the end of April

* Health & Human Services will also **suspend foreclosures and evictions**

* Navy hospital ships are being **deployed to impacted areas**

* America and Canada will**halt all non-essential travel** across their border

**President Trump** held a phone meeting today with 130 CEOs to hear directly what they need to respond to this crisis. Defeating this virus will take a historic partnership between our public and private sectors. The Trump Administration is leading that charge.

**🎬****President Trump:** [With Defense Production Act, more resources will be mobilized](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=7058b27ea0&e=3ee4985eea)

**History has shown** that our nation always rises to the occasion when staring down uncertain and trying times. The American people are proving that once again now—and the President couldn’t be prouder, Vice President Mike Pence wrote in _USA Today_.

**“In the weeks ahead,** President Trump and this administration will continue to take strong action to protect the American people, and we will continue to build on the partnerships that we’ve forged with state and local governments, as well as with the scientific and business communities,” he said.

**“But for all of these** important steps, there is no substitute for the action of the American people.”

**MUST READ:** [“VP: We need the American people’s help to fight coronavirus now”](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=8d98ed7dd0&e=3ee4985eea)

---
|
---
|
## **🎬** **Health experts: Social distancing works!**
---
| [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=eac7934c60&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| **This week,** President Trump issued a national call to action: Use the next 15 days to slow the spread of Coronavirus and protect our most vulnerable citizens from illness.

**Many of the** common-sense steps he outlined are simple, such as washing your hands regularly, avoiding large gatherings, and working from home if possible. But as health experts explain, small actions by each of us can lead to a massive collective impact.

[Do your part – slow the spread now!](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=1c1e2261ff&e=3ee4985eea)

**Social distancing,** for example, is one of the most important actions every American can take. Dr. Deborah Birx, the Administration’s Coronavirus Response Coordinator, says that social distancing is “what we refer to when we ask people to stay six feet apart.”

**Why six feet apart?** Because scientific evidence shows “that’s how far your droplets can go when you sneeze or cough,” Dr. Birx says.

**🎬** [Surgeon General: Why millennials should take Coronavirus seriously](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=6306510ec8&e=3ee4985eea)

|

|

name: Holcomb Crew
subject: One Indiana: how to stay healthy
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Eric Holcomb for Indiana
body:
It’s a difficult time. Everyone is doing what they can to slow the spread of the coronavirus, or COVID-19, and keep their loved ones--and each other- Friend,

Across the state, Hoosiers are all in this together -- making changes as we work to slow the spread of the coronavirus, or COVID-19. It’s all about keeping Hoosiers safe...especially our most vulnerable Hoosiers _(and “flattening the curve,” as the experts say). _
Governor Holcomb -- working continuously with the Indiana State Department of Health, top medical professionals from across the state and nation, and local, state and federal partners that are working together on this -- wanted us to share a few guidelines with you in this week’s One Indiana to help you stay healthy during this global pandemic.
* **Take more time at home.**
* Following Governor Holcomb & the CDC's directive, large gatherings should be postponed.
* If possible, work from home -- and consider videoconferencing for meetings.
* If you have to be out, practice safe social distancing.
* **Say hello with an elbow bump instead of a handshake.**
* **Wash your hands!**
* Frequently wash your hands with soap and water for at least 20 seconds.
* **[Here’s a Hoosier way to make sure you’re washing long enough.](http://links.holcombforindiana.com/u/click?_t=60bbc6d769fe4010b248f63c6cb0e872&_m=4b7f6951c5a9444b8514ced130e6b4f5&_e=7ruFb_KvpZH_9PLcDQLsus2h0UJlLjLoSVJAj_WRVc39FfTYWDq3vwB6ew8HXbWu4bxU9iedDvktc9tc4133iX4K4GHnnFrr6yI0GWaaxnUT-kfSDrkNxK4n9qeit3kv30iiNffGUsYtVnpzlpWtoWjGK3Toe7eN-dCseiOy98HGUq7bcLSaqZIkQZQaukXuH5n01V625J-aWzGWmH4pWs5nH777IJgtomyqjlO9GVp0sMo8RGIe7pDs0ph6pe1sFUhKvFNe7EUI_By7azF5zkQPiCSfLpdQXq2LZ7SVL_GryJht0opjZrTKr_-tE5_-mDZUKO24VVhWVc7Xoa54wPbAXDlcFYhN0rfsY6EkNGYifS4-u0c72H1VXr-9PzHwcEIa5DrPO5LyYI8qm2qjWgswp99tONWPxOVsXRD3B439AWGvbo5ThtNWNkZrI01L)**
* If soap and water are not readily available, clean your hands with a hand sanitizer that contains at least 60% alcohol.
* **Avoid touching your eyes, nose, and mouth with unwashed hands.**
* **Make sure to disinfect and clean all frequently touched surfaces — phones, keyboards, light switches, doorknobs, etc.**
* **If you’re sick, stay home.**
* Call ahead before visiting your doctor.
* Seek medical attention if your condition worsens.

**As Governor Holcomb said, it’ll take all of us -- all of us -- each doing our part to keep Hoosiers healthy and safe during this outbreak.

** You can get the most up-to-date information on Indiana’s coronavirus response at **[Indiana State Department of Health’s website.](http://links.holcombforindiana.com/u/click?_t=60bbc6d769fe4010b248f63c6cb0e872&_m=4b7f6951c5a9444b8514ced130e6b4f5&_e=7ruFb_KvpZH_9PLcDQLsuuCVU2pyu1XgLe6ig92LT2bQ_B7brUzsCxLW4i0LcXojVE8dcowjuI4e2nI8NHdJJ2xfdwhpYQXacghH6s4M0VxxrB216aw3q3sfTi5-Jp_A0zYr7szG3KIgCeqmrteXDJF7UcDVW1GtkavJrz_OnC3uvXZ1Sp1D0-FX5skdBBpttNESWSgPxBf35i1Km4IVXHrCxZbkfJaY8SGii6tV6VlmcwKoggv5ncWZ23y3u6j-ew5grgkMsT3jBA9DB_K6ZCZcobL6Ucb0RiKNjHW7BXOKiRLUGNDKWNhKyuH_qdGKBPULQ79aQl8tcrWY102z3QrxgBpZJhmg0wTuaUDMRVBkhd_Kgv88UGupOAHqLDeWag82GKaRaxdHX8ZHcDLfSA%3D%3D)** Thanks,

| | | Dear Friends, Family, and Voters, Since launching my campaign in October I’ve had the privilege and honor to meet with so many amazing people. This journey has been a huge blessing for me and my family. Lisa and I are so thankful for the opportunity to share our vision and values with so many of you. As you’ve began to know me over the last few months I hope that you’ve learned a few things. I’m a follower of Christ and I love my family. I believe honesty, integrity, and transparency are the necessary principles for governance. Lastly, Illinoisans should have hope for strong conservative leadership. I’ve ran a campaign on a few ideals: defending the unborn, supporting President Trump, and preserving our freedom to secure a better America for future generations. No matter what, it’s most important to me to stand up for what is right. Sometimes you win and sometimes you do not. I stood up for conservative and Christian principles. I stood up for my values and mission to serve. It was an honor to participate in this race. I’m so proud of what we’ve accomplished as a team. Thank you to everyone who has supported us in this campaign. We’re so thankful for your generous time, money, and prayers and we can’t thank you enough. Lastly, I’d like to congratulate Jim Oberweis on his victory. He has my full support in the general election and I believe he will be a great ambassador for conservative values in our District. I’m looking forward to helping him take back the 14th District. God bless, Jerry, Lisa, & Michael Evans

clean_body: | My team and I just ran the numbers again — and here’s the deal: Our campaign needs a surge of grassroots support to make up for our missed fundraising goal and to get us back on track for our first FEC deadline of the year. Will you pitch in to help? name: Rev. Raphael Warnock
subject: Donate to the Giving Kitchen
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Warnock for Georgia
body:
| | |
---
Friend: These are difficult times, and workers across the nation are feeling the physical and economic strains of this public health crisis.
In many states, including here in Georgia, the food service industry is being hit especially hard. Many local businesses and restaurants are closing for the foreseeable future, laying off or sending workers home without pay. Food service workers are a critical part of our society and our economy, and we must stand with them in their hour of need.
[**And so I 'm asking you to look out for those less fortunate than yourself, and to serve those who so selflessly serve others by joining me and making a donation to The Giving Kitchen.**](https://secure.warnockforgeorgia.com/page/m/60b3e6f7/288179be/a8dfc0a/348eeb52/3458210785/VEsH/?g=3wiJLcQot1BeM8zu13FVD5A "https://connect.clickandpledge.com/w/Form/d11bff52-0cd0-44d8-9403-465614e4f342?source=rwarnock_nd")
The Giving Kitchen is a non-profit organization here in Georgia that provides emergency assistance to food service workers through financial support and a network of community resources. **Your donation will help ensure that a food service worker is able to put food on their own table and will help connect them with critical resources needed at this time.**
It is during these times of hardship that Americans prove their resiliency and capacity for unity by coming together to support one another, especially those who are most affected. We all have an obligation to look out for one another now more than ever.
[**Please join me in making a contribution to the Giving Kitchen today.**](https://secure.warnockforgeorgia.com/page/m/60b3e6f7/288179be/a8dfc0a/348eeb52/3458210785/VEsE/?g=3wiJLcQot1BeM8zu13FVD5A "https://connect.clickandpledge.com/w/Form/d11bff52-0cd0-44d8-9403-465614e4f342?source=rwarnock_nd")

- Rev. Raphael Warnock
